required_attachments_for_your_session.note: Do NOT attach the GUIDE_*.xml or GUIDE_*.md files. Those are Guide-instance scope. Do NOT attach Stage 0/1/3/4/5/6 XMLs.

ground_truth_at_handoff.both_trees_clean: git status --porcelain returned empty on both repos at handoff.
ground_truth_at_handoff.fleet_state: state.json.fleet_paused = true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by "copilot_agent". DO NOT resume — fleet stays paused through Stage 4 cutover per Master Plan.
ground_truth_at_handoff.vikunja_server: STOPPED. 0 vikunja* processes. Stage-0.4 invariant in effect. Restart only if Stage 2 work explicitly requires Vikunja (it should not — Stage 2 is code refactor, not data ops).
ground_truth_at_handoff.scheduled_tasks: 13/13 BlarAI tasks Disabled. Do not Enable any of them.
ground_truth_at_handoff.worktrees: git worktree list returns only main. Do not create new worktrees.
ground_truth_at_handoff.mcp_configs: Still point to BlarAI. Stage 4 will repoint them. Do not touch.

ea_handoff.comprehension_gate_reminder: The Stage 2 XML opens with a <comprehension_gate> block. You MUST present the structured summary (objective / work items / files / verification / risks) and then STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval before any edits, branches, or commands. Stage 2 is HIGH risk. The LA + Guide will strictly enforce the wait-for-approval rule. Pre-execution acknowledgment skipping = comprehension gate REJECTED (per Guide handoff decision d3).
ea_handoff.fleet_pause_sop_reminder: Fleet is already paused at handoff (last_updated_by "copilot_agent"). For Stage 2's commit you do NOT need to re-pause — the pause persists. But before YOUR commit on `chore/platform-extraction`: 1. Verify state.json.fleet_paused is still true (`python -c "import json; print(json.load(open('tools/autonomy_budget/state.json'))['fleet_paused'])"` -> True). 2. If a parallel agent ever flipped it to false during your session, re-pause via `state.pause_fleet('stage 2 refactor', updated_by='copilot_agent', path='C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')` and commit `chore(ops): pause fleet -- stage 2 refactor` BEFORE your refactor commit. 3. Do NOT call state.resume_fleet at end of session. Fleet stays paused. Function-name reminder: `state.resume_fleet()` — NEVER `unpause_fleet()` (raises AttributeError).
ea_handoff.between_stages_cleanup_summary_what_chan:
Between Stage 1 close and your start, the LA authorized 7 cleanup items (C1-C7) that landed in two commits: BlarAI aa09f6a, devplatform 3894221. The items relevant to YOUR Stage 2 work:
The `_project_context.py` Python snippet you will write at item 2.2 has been generalized. The version in the XML now uses: name_key = f"{root.name.lower()}_project_id" return registry.get(name_key) instead of the original `registry.get("blarai")`. Reason: registry.yaml schema is `blarai_project_id: 3`, not `blarai: 3`. The original code would have raised at first runtime call. Paste the corrected XML version verbatim.
A new <vikunja_access_protocol> block has been added documenting MCP-preferred / REST-fallback patterns and naming `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json` `mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS` as the canonical credential source. Stage 2 is mostly code refactor and should not need Vikunja calls; if it does, follow this protocol. Both paths are byte-equivalent server-side.
The PowerShell preflight that resolves `$BLARAI_PID` from `.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml` now uses regex `'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)'`. Original `'blarai:\s*(\d+)'` would have returned no match. Use the corrected XML version.
`agent_gates_bus` (vikunja_project_id 6) and `architecture_decisions` (vikunja_project_id 5) were promoted from `blarai_auxiliary_projects` to top-level `projects.*`. Three remaining auxiliaries (4, 7, 9) carry "Verify at Stage 6" annotations. If your refactor reads registry.yaml, the schema is now: `projects.blarai`, `projects.devplatform_meta`, `projects.fleet_reports`, `projects.agent_gates_bus`, `projects.architecture_decisions`, plus `blarai_auxiliary_projects.*`. Your `_project_context.resolve()` uses `name_key = "blarai_project_id"` which lives under `projects.blarai.vikunja_project_id` (NOT under the new top-level promotions). No code change needed — just be aware the registry shape evolved.
Anomalies A6-A10 added (all LOW). They document the cleanup itself; no action required from you.

critical_watchpoints_carried_from_stage1.watchpoint: Stage 1 Anomaly A4 (STATUS.md) flagged that BlarAI has 6 Vikunja projects, not 1. After cleanup C4/C6, three remain in `blarai_auxiliary_projects` (4 BlarAI Infrastructure, 7 BlarAI Fleet Dashboard, 9 BlarAI Drafts). Your Stage 2 XML item 2.2 follow-up directive says: "Stage 2 (refactor seam) should confirm whether any tool needs to address the auxiliary projects; if so, extend `blarai.yaml` schema with a `secondary_project_ids:` list and update `_project_context.py`." Quick assessment expected. No fleet runtime tool currently aggregates across BlarAI projects, so the answer is most likely "no extension needed at Stage 2; defer to Stage 6". Document your finding in your Stage 2 commit message or a STATUS anomaly if you decide differently.

Stage 2 is the single highest-leverage stage. It introduces the multi-project seam BEFORE anything physically moves. Defaults MUST preserve current behavior — running fleet tools with no arguments must produce identical output to today. Stage 2 is reversible only via `git revert`; there is no stage-internal rollback. Test defaults preservation explicitly per the verify block in each work_item.

Item 2.4.v2 added 8 additional tools to the refactor list (path corrections per R12). Two of them — `la_merge_approve.ps1` (G-1/G-4) and the four `tools/scheduled-tasks/*.ps1` watchdogs — are invoked OUTSIDE the wake_launcher harness, so XML-injected `--project-root` will not save them. They need explicit `-BlarAIRoot` parameter with env fallback signature: `param([string]$BlarAIRoot = $(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' }), ...)`. Do not skip these.

commit_discipline.branch: Stay on `chore/platform-extraction`. Do not create new branches. Do not merge to main during Stage 2.

forbidden.item: Do not commit to devplatform repo (Stage 2 is BlarAI-side only — devplatform/tools/ gets populated at Stage 3, not Stage 2).
Do not edit other stage XMLs (0, 1, 3, 4, 5, 6) under any circumstance.
Do not edit STATUS.md anomalies A1-A10 (read-only history). NEW anomalies for Stage 2 issues append as A11+, with the format used by A4-A10 in STATUS.md.
Do not touch state.json beyond the pause-verify check above.
Do not start Vikunja unless a Stage 2 work item explicitly requires it (none should).

commit_discipline.required_status_md_updates: At Stage 2 close, append: 1. New row in the Stage Ledger for Stage 2 with date, BlarAI commit hash, devplatform column "—" (no devplatform commit this stage), and key evidence. 2. New entry in the Execution Log with the format established by prior entries. 3. Any anomalies as A11, A12, ... appended after A10, using the same heading + bullet shape. 4. Update the "Next stage to execute" row at the top to **Stage 3 — Copy tools**.

verification_before_signaling_complete.cmd: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git status --porcelain # MUST be empty after commit
cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git status --porcelain # MUST be empty (no devplatform changes this stage)
Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' | Where-Object State -ne 'Disabled' | Measure-Object # MUST be 0 (all 13 tasks still Disabled)
git worktree list # MUST list only main
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git log --oneline -1 # capture new Stage 2 commit hash for the Stage Ledger row

Run the <verify> block of every work_item 2.1 through 2.10 (and the v2 items 2.4.v2 / 2.5.v2 / 2.7.v2). Each verify is a defaults-preservation smoke test — running the tool with no args must produce today's behavior.

stop_conditions.condition: If any verify in V8 fails to preserve default behavior, STOP. Do not commit. Report to Guide / LA.
If `_vikunja_client.py` cannot enforce `project_id` on all call sites without breaking existing fleet tools, STOP. This is the Master Plan §D.1 rule and is non-negotiable.
If you discover the registry.yaml shape (post-cleanup) doesn't match what the XML assumes, STOP and flag a new anomaly. Do NOT silently adapt the code.
If you exhaust your context window before completing the full Stage 2 scope, STOP. Do not partial-commit. Report to Guide for a clean handoff to a successor EA.

ea_handoff.closing_handoff_to_la: On Stage 2 completion, your final message to the LA must include: - Stage 2 BlarAI commit hash - Outputs of V1-V7 (concise) - Per-work-item V8 result summary (PASS / observed-output-snippet) - Decision on watchpoint w_A4_auxiliaries (extension at Stage 2 vs deferred to Stage 6) - Any A11+ anomalies recorded - Confirmation that fleet remains paused, Vikunja remains stopped, scheduled tasks remain Disabled, no worktrees created
